name: Face Swap Evaluation Metric Crosswalk v1
purpose: Separate nine commonly cited face-swap evaluation measurements by reference, direction, aggregation level, required evidence and interpretation limit.

scope.subject: Face-swap output evaluation and deepfake-detector interpretation
scope.primaryGenerationBenchmark: CASIA FaceSwapping benchmark published with arXiv:2605.00883
scope.detectorGeneralizationReference: Real-World FaceSwap dataset study published as arXiv:2502.10920

method.sourceBoundary: Metric definitions and protocol roles were normalized from the cited primary papers and official benchmark repositories. No third-party leaderboard values were copied.
normalizationRules: Every metric names the reference object it compares with the output.
Sample-level, video-level and dataset-level measurements remain distinct.
Higher or lower direction is stated only inside the documented protocol.
Identity transfer is kept separate from target-attribute preservation.
Distribution similarity is not converted into a score for one image.
Detector performance or confidence is not presented as perceptual realism, identity fidelity or generation quality.
No metric value is attributed to FaceSwapAI unless a reproducible production measurement has been published.

metrics.name: Identity Retrieval
metrics.comparison: Output face features against a labeled gallery of source identities
metrics.answers: How often the correct source identity is retrieved at the stated rank under one fixed protocol.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Whether one person is definitively identified, whether the output looks realistic or whether target attributes were preserved.

metrics.name: Identity Similarity
metrics.answers: How close the source and output features are under one stated recognition encoder.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Target pose, expression, background preservation, perceptual realism or an identity claim independent of the encoder.

metrics.name: Pose Error
metrics.comparison: Estimated output head pose against estimated target head pose
metrics.answers: How closely the output follows target head orientation under the stated estimator.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Source identity fidelity, expression preservation or visual integration.

metrics.name: Expression Error
metrics.comparison: Estimated output expression parameters against estimated target expression parameters
metrics.answers: How closely the output follows target expression under the stated estimator.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Source identity fidelity, pose preservation, lip synchronization or naturalness.

metrics.name: Frechet Inception Distance
metrics.comparison: Feature distribution of generated outputs against a stated reference distribution
metrics.answers: How close two feature distributions are under one implementation and sampling protocol.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: The quality of one image, source identity transfer or target-attribute preservation.

metrics.name: Structural Similarity
metrics.comparison: Aligned output against a corresponding protocol reference or ground truth
metrics.answers: How similar aligned luminance, contrast and structure are under the stated setup.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Source identity transfer, unpaired realism or whether a minimally changed target is a successful swap.

metrics.name: Subject Consistency
metrics.comparison: Subject features across frames of the generated video
metrics.reference: Other sampled frames in the same output video
metrics.answers: How stable subject features remain over time under the stated video evaluator.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Whether the subject matches the source identity, whether lip sync is correct or whether every frame is artifact-free.

metrics.name: Background Consistency
metrics.comparison: Background features across frames of the generated video
metrics.reference: Other sampled frames in the same output video
metrics.answers: How stable scene-background features remain over time under the stated video evaluator.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Whether non-face pixels are unchanged, whether the swapped face is correct or whether source identity is stable.

metrics.name: Deepfake Detector AUROC or Confidence
metrics.evaluationLevel: dataset for AUROC; individual detector output for confidence
metrics.comparison: Detector predictions against labeled real and manipulated samples
metrics.direction: higher AUROC is better detector discrimination; per-output confidence has no universal generation-quality direction
metrics.answers: How one detector separates labeled classes on the stated distribution, or how that detector scores one output.
metrics.doesNotAnswer: Perceptual realism, identity fidelity, consent, safety or quality across another detector or post-processing pipeline.

reviewChecklist: Name the output, source, target, gallery or reference set used by each metric.
Publish evaluator versions, preprocessing, sample counts and aggregation rules.
Keep raw directions and values instead of silently collapsing unlike metrics into one score.
Add blinded human review for identity, target attributes, integration, occlusion and normal-speed video continuity.
Report failures, exclusions and unavailable outputs instead of replacing them silently.
Re-run a protocol after a material model, provider or preprocessing change.

officialReferences.title: Towards High Fidelity Face Swapping: A Comprehensive Survey and New Benchmark
officialReferences.role: Primary face-swapping benchmark and eight-metric protocol.

officialReferences.title: CASIA FaceSwapping benchmark repository
officialReferences.role: Pair protocols, evaluator implementations and metric directions.

officialReferences.title: Do Deepfake Detectors Work in Reality?
officialReferences.role: Evidence that detector generalization changes under real-world domain shift and super-resolution post-processing.

officialReferences.title: GANs Trained by a Two Time-Scale Update Rule Converge to a Local Nash Equilibrium
officialReferences.publisher: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems

officialReferences.title: Image Quality Assessment: From Error Visibility to Structural Similarity
VBench: Comprehensive Benchmark Suite for Video Generative Models
officialReferences.role: Evaluator used for subject and background consistency in the CASIA benchmark.

limitations: This crosswalk is a dated reading aid, not a provider leaderboard, model certification or claim that one metric suite is sufficient for every use case.
The dataset contains definitions and interpretation boundaries, not generated faces, private media, embeddings, model weights or benchmark scores.
Metric implementations can differ from the cited examples; values are comparable only when the complete protocol matches.
The cited papers and repositories describe their own datasets and evaluators, not FaceSwapAI production quality.
Human preference, accessibility, consent, legal rights and publication context are separate decisions that numeric quality metrics do not settle.
